The river of filthy water represents "the depths of hell" (1 Nephi 12:16;15:29).

Nephi now returns to the imagery of Lehi’s dream. The depths of the filthy water represent the depths of hell, the mists of darkness represent the temptations of the devil, the large and spacious building represents the vain imaginations and pride of the world, and the great and terrible gulf represents the word of the justice of the Eternal God. This amazing coincidentia oppositorum is the clash of black and white. With the Arabs, to be white of countenance is to be blessed and to be black of countenance is to be cursed; there are parallel expressions in Hebrew and Egyptian. And what of Lehi's people?
